metadata.description: Finite Impulse Response (FIR) basis HRF with time binning and indicator functions

semantic_description.purpose: Validate FIR (Finite Impulse Response) basis HRF generator with time binning and indicator functions
semantic_description.algorithm:
FIR basis provides maximum flexibility for HRF modeling by dividing time into discrete bins:
**Mathematical Foundation:** - Divides temporal window [0, span] into nbasis equal bins - Each basis function is an indicator function for one time bin - Bin width = span / nbasis - Bin assignment: bin_index = floor(t / bin_width) + 1 (1-indexed)
**Implementation Logic:** For each time point t: 1. If t < 0 or t ≥ span: assign to no bin (all zeros) 2. If 0 ≤ t < span: assign to bin = floor(t / bin_width) + 1 3. Special case: t = 0 maps to bin 1 4. Boundary: t = span maps to no bin (outside domain)
**Basis Function Properties:** - Each B_i(t) ∈ {0, 1} (binary indicator) - Support of B_i = [(i-1) * bin_width, i * bin_width) - Σ B_i(t) = 1 for t ∈ [0, span), 0 otherwise - Basis functions are mutually exclusive (non-overlapping)
**Output Matrix:** - Dimensions: length(t) × nbasis - Each row contains exactly one 1 and (nbasis-1) zeros for valid t - Invalid t (outside [0, span)) produce rows of all zeros
semantic_description.mathematical_properties:
**FIR Basis Properties:** - Binary values: all entries ∈ {0, 1} - Mutual exclusivity: only one basis active per time point - Partition of time: each valid t maps to exactly one bin - Rectangular basis functions with sharp boundaries - No smoothness constraints (discontinuous at bin boundaries)
**Temporal Binning:** - Bin i covers interval [(i-1) * bin_width, i * bin_width) - Uniform bin widths: bin_width = span / nbasis - Left-inclusive, right-exclusive intervals - No gaps or overlaps between bins
**Matrix Properties:** - Binary matrix: all entries ∈ {0, 1} - Row sums = 1 for t ∈ [0, span), 0 otherwise - Each column has finite support (one contiguous interval) - Sparse matrix structure for large time vectors
semantic_description.edge_cases: - t < 0: Should map to no bin (row of zeros) - t ≥ span: Should map to no bin (row of zeros) - t = 0: Should map to bin 1 - t = span: Should map to no bin (boundary exclusion) - Single bin (nbasis = 1): All valid t map to bin 1 - Very fine binning: Many bins with narrow widths - Boundary values: t exactly at bin boundaries

input.name: time_vector
input.description: Time points from 0 to 24 seconds with 0.1 second intervals

implementations.R:
# R reference implementation # Function: hrf_fir_generator(nbasis = 12, span = 24) # Location: R/hrf.R lines 856-893
# Test default parameters fir_default <- hrf_fir_generator(nbasis = 12, span = 24) time_vec <- seq(0, 24, by = 0.1) result_default <- fir_default(time_vec)
# Test custom parameters fir_custom <- hrf_fir_generator(nbasis = 8, span = 16) result_custom <- fir_custom(seq(0, 16, by = 0.5))
# Test fine binning fir_fine <- hrf_fir_generator(nbasis = 20, span = 10) result_fine <- fir_fine(seq(0, 9.9, by = 0.1))
# Test single bin fir_single <- hrf_fir_generator(nbasis = 1, span = 24) result_single <- fir_single(seq(0, 23.9, by = 1))
# Test boundary conditions boundary_times <- c(-1, 0, 2, 4, 6, 12, 24, 25) result_boundary <- fir_default(boundary_times)
# Test specific bin assignments test_times <- c(0, 1, 2, 12) result_specific <- fir_default(test_times)
implementations.Python:
# Python implementation following semantic specification
import numpy as np
def hrf_fir_generator(nbasis=12, span=24): """ Create FIR basis HRF generator with time binning.
Args: nbasis: Number of time bins (default: 12) span: Temporal window [0, span) (default: 24)
Returns: Function that creates FIR basis matrix for given time points """ def fir_basis(t): """ Generate FIR basis matrix for time points t.
Args: t: Time points (scalar or array)
Returns: Matrix with rows=len(t), columns=nbasis """ t = np.asarray(t, dtype=float).flatten() n_time = len(t)
# Initialize output matrix basis_matrix = np.zeros((n_time, nbasis), dtype=float)
# Calculate bin width bin_width = span / nbasis
for i, time in enumerate(t): if 0 <= time < span: # Calculate bin index (0-based) if time == 0: bin_idx = 0 # Special case: t=0 maps to first bin else: bin_idx = int(np.floor(time / bin_width))
# Ensure bin index is within bounds bin_idx = min(bin_idx, nbasis - 1) basis_matrix[i, bin_idx] = 1.0 # else: time outside [0, span) remains all zeros
return basis_matrix
return fir_basis
# Default parameter wrapper def hrf_fir_default(): return hrf_fir_generator(nbasis=12, span=24)
